summary: Iteration 115 backend + lightweight frontend smoke. Backend: 12 passed / 1 skipped — all iteration 113 regressions PASS and all new iteration 115 behaviors (service_time persisted on order, travel seats optional, travel_time auto-fill from route.departure_time, user-provided service_time wins, 409 on seat overlap) PASS. Frontend smoke: /transactions fully works (all 6 data-testids + list/grid/details switching); /receipts shows all 3 testids; /orders and /admin/bills did not expose testids in one run despite source wiring them correctly — likely a loading/render timing issue worth a spot-check. Walk-in modal testids confirmed in source; runtime open-click attempt failed to locate the Walk-in button in one run.

minor.issue: Router is declared with prefix='/api/operator/manual-bookings' and the POST decorator uses '/', which works (trailing-slash required). There is no longer a dual @router.post('') entry, so callers MUST include the trailing slash or will hit 307.

When seats are omitted, booking_details.seats_assigned=false and seat_numbers=[] as required, but seat_bookings collection is (correctly) not written. Consumers that read from seat_bookings won't see headcount-only walk-ins — make sure reports/occupancy pull both seat_bookings and order.booking_details.passengers for on_site bookings.

booking_details stores both travel_time and departure_time set to the same resolved value. Ticket/enrichment consumers should prefer travel_time (user-provided wins) to avoid stale route.departure_time when an operator overrides the time.

integration_issues.issue: In the runtime smoke check, data-testids [quick-date-range-trigger], [view-mode-toggle], [operator-scope-filter] were NOT found on /orders or /admin/bills even after 4s wait, while /transactions and /receipts rendered them correctly. Source code in Orders.jsx and BillsManagement.jsx wires these components unconditionally — this looks like a page-level render/loading issue (possibly error during initial fetch). Main agent should open /orders and /admin/bills as admin and confirm the toolbar renders even when no data is returned.

Automated click could not locate the 'Walk-in Booking' button on /management/travel during smoke. Source TravelManagement.jsx line 615 has onClick->setIsWalkInOpen(true) with a 'Walk-in Booking' label so the button does exist. The page may gate the button behind a services tab/operator role check — please re-verify manually. Modal testids are present in source: walkin-service-time, walkin-passengers, walkin-seat-numbers, walkin-submit-btn.

action_items: Verify /orders (admin role) and /admin/bills render the QuickDateRangeFilter + ViewModeToggle + OperatorScopeFilter toolbar on first load — smoke failed to find testids despite correct source wiring; likely a render/loading timing or JS-error issue.

Confirm the 'Walk-in Booking' button on /management/travel is visible for admin role (not only operator). Consider adding a data-testid on the button itself (e.g. data-testid='open-walkin-modal') to make the flow easier to automate.
Consider fixing the submit button testid spelling: iteration 115 request names it 'walkin-submit' but source uses 'walkin-submit-btn' — align between docs, tests, and code.
Optional: update WalkIn submit docs to note that seats are optional and travel_time auto-fills; the backend behavior is verified.
critical_code_review_comments: manual_bookings.py POST: when seats are omitted for travel, no row is written to seat_bookings and order.booking_details.seats_assigned=false — correct per spec, but any seat-map/availability UI that renders from seat_bookings alone will UNDERCOUNT walk-in headcount-only bookings. Please make sure availability calculations include order.booking_details.passengers for channel=='on_site' travel orders.

manual_bookings.py: scheduled_time precedence is `data.service_time OR booking_details.travel_time OR svc.departure_time` — this matches the spec (user wins over route default). booking_details.departure_time is also set to the same resolved value which can mislead readers into thinking it reflects the route's canonical schedule. Prefer only travel_time on the order (or rename to avoid confusion).
_find_linked_customer now uses re.escape(email) — good fix, resolves the regex-injection issue flagged in iteration 113.

Travel walk-in flow still does not set customer_linked / notify behavior when seats are omitted — it does, but only because the linked_user_id path runs regardless of seats, which is correct. Just confirming no regression.

Orders.jsx / BillsManagement.jsx / Receipts.jsx / Transactions.jsx: shared filter components are imported identically, but Orders.jsx has `operator-scope-filter` rendered only inside `{isAllOrdersView && ...}` while the spec states 'Operator filter visible only for admin' — this will ALSO hide it for admin viewing their personal /orders (not /admin/orders). Confirm that admin users land on `isAllOrdersView=true` when visiting /orders, or document the two admin routes.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 executed, 1 skipped for missing operator-owned non-travel service)
success_rate.frontend: partial smoke — /transactions PASS, /receipts PASS, /orders and /admin/bills inconclusive, walk-in modal not opened

seed_data_creation: Created TEST travel walk-in orders on 2030-06-15, 2030-08-10 (no seats), 2030-08-11 (auto-time), 2030-08-12 (seats+time). All orders use random seat prefixes (T/V/I + 4 hex) and far-future dates so they never collide with real data. Also created dedupe notification TEST_iter113_* via regression test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_iteration115_walkin_service_time.py + test_iteration113_walkin_notif_vehicle.py for regression. Both files use session-scoped fixtures and operator's own travel route. operator_id must be read from user.operator_id OR user.operator_context.operator_id. Walk-in POST requires trailing slash (/api/operator/manual-bookings/). Walk-in modal testids: walkin-service-time, walkin-passengers, walkin-seat-numbers, walkin-submit-btn (NOT walkin-submit). Shared toolbar testids live in components/common/{QuickDateRangeFilter,ViewModeToggle,OperatorScopeFilter}.jsx — Orders.jsx hides OperatorScopeFilter behind isAllOrdersView.

feature_verdict.iter115_travel_walkin_without_seats: PASS (order_id returned, seats_assigned=false, seat_numbers=[], passengers preserved)
feature_verdict.iter115_non_travel_service_time_persist: SKIPPED (operator owns no restaurant/hotel in seed)
feature_verdict.frontend_transactions_page: PASS (all 6 testids + view-mode list/grid/details switching works)
feature_verdict.frontend_orders_page: INCONCLUSIVE (testids not found at runtime despite correct source wiring)
feature_verdict.frontend_admin_bills_page: INCONCLUSIVE (testids not found at runtime despite correct source wiring)
feature_verdict.frontend_walkin_modal_open: INCONCLUSIVE (modal not opened in automation; testids confirmed in source)
